---
title: "Lesson1_Intro_to_R"
author: "Paul Lott"
date: "2024-05-13"
output: html_notebook
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

The plan for the R/RStudio portion of the course is to provide an
overview of RStudio, R Datatypes, Conditionals, Loops, Functions, and
common R libraries. We unfortunately don't have enough time to make you
an expert at R. The goal is to get you to be proficient in RStudio on
the HIVE Cluster.

# 1. Introduction to R

R is not only a programming language, but also a free software
environment for statistical computing and graphics. In the past 20
years, R has been adopted in the fields of bioinformatics and data
analysis as a fundamental tool.

R is an interpreted language (meaning it's not compiled until you run
it) - this makes it easy to share, download, and run script files from
others. R provides a command-line interface and a Graphical User
Interface. However, Third-party editors like **Rstudio** provide
additional utilities that make it easier to program and use R.

If you want to download and run R/Rstudio on your own computer:

-   [Download R](https://cran.r-project.org/)

-   [Download RStudio
    Desktop](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop)

## Allocating an Rstudio Instance on HIVE Ondemand

1.  Login to HIVE OnDemand and select RStudio Server from the
    Interactive Apps

![](images/clipboard-495375131.png){width="189"}

2.  Provision the required amount of resources:

![](images/clipboard-418530672.png){width="407"}

3.  Click **"Launch"**
4.  In "My Interactive Sessions", click on **"Connect to RStudio
    Server"** after it has been setup

## Very Quick overview of the Original R

Let's take a quick look at the R GUI/ R Command Line Console.

![](https://users.ssc.wisc.edu/~hemken/Rworkshops/interface/RInterface.JPG)

The R Console is where we can execute R commands. However, you'll notice
that it's not a great place to keep track of Notes, Details, Code, or
Plots.

## R Example Dataset

R has a lot of small example datasets available.
[Datasets](https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/datasets/html/00Index.html)

The Graphical console is very basic and limited.

The non-graphical console is even more basic and limited.

# Don't Use R Gui/Console unless you need to.

For reproducibility, you need something better - where we can test,
develop, and document our project code.

This is where **RStudio** really shines. OK - Let's take a look at
**RStudio** in Lesson 2.

##
